mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 260 in the CPIAUCSL series signals central bank inaction on price pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as real rates rise.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 260 despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicates robust demand conditions, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through earnings optimism.
failure_condition: Rank IC for combined GDPC1/INDPRO signals falls below 0.05 over any 3-year window, or 60-day returns show no correlation with demand indicators.

mechanism_narrative: High unemployment (UNRATE > 10%) combined with weak job growth (PAYEMS < 100k) signals a slack labor market, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through earnings pessimism.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE > 10% and PAYEMS < 100k despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strong manufacturing activity, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through sectoral momentum.
failure_condition: INDPRO shows no correlation with 5-day SPX returns over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST > 1200k) indicate resilient demand for construction, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through sectoral spillovers.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where HOUST exceeds 1200 despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: Low FEDFUNDS combined with high RSAFS indicates accommodative policy and strong consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through multiple expansion.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS/RSAFS signals falls below 0.05 over any 3-year window, or 60-day returns show no correlation with monetary-demand indicators.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in PAYEMS signals weakening labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through investor risk-off behavior.
failure_condition: PAYEMS shows no correlation with 5-day SPX returns over any 1-year rolling window.
